title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 15.
content:
There was an Old Man in a boat,
    Who said, "I'm afloat! I'm afloat!"
    When they said, "No! you ain't!"
    He was ready to faint,
    That unhappy Old Man in a boat.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 15.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
